transcript: [accented] Yeah, I need to talk to Gomez, and stuff.
This is not the Gomez residence.
This number has been ours for over a year, but we get Gomez calls all the time.
[accented] No, I am Gomez, I said.
[accented] I need to know what my call activity has been over the last, uh, 15 or 18 months.
Well, why do you need to know that? Why... why do I have your telephone number and I keep getting all your phone calls?
[accented] Ah... that's exactly why I'm calling. I... frankly, I don't like the attitude that you've been giving my, uh... my customers, you know.
I, uh... excuse me, this is my residence. All I said was...
[accented] But... but, this is my business, you know.
Listen-- no one ever said it was a business. They call, they ask for Gomez.
So-and-so Gomez, by a lady's name, usually.
[accented] Don't... don't talk to me about my business, young lady. Okay? That's what I have to say to you.
M... Mr. Gomez, if you have a problem because people are calling me for you, you need to call the phone company.
Which is what I intend to do as soon as I hang up the phone.
[accented] No, you know, look... you... you, you give me problems about my business.
Uh, no. Excuse me, I don't give you problems about your business. When people call here, they ask for... Ruben Gomez or some other woman, I say "I'm sorry, this isn't the Gomez residence."
I don't know what you do for a business. I could care less.
[accented] I deal with Elastico Gomez, ma'am.
[accented] What is your name, you know?
What's the difference? This is my phone number, I don't need to give you any information. This is the number that was assigned to me more than a year ago.
[accented] You know, I have many people in my line of work, you know.

I don't care. All I'm saying is, we get phone calls all the time for Gomez, we always say "this isn't the Gomez residence, we've had this number for about a year now... and I don't know who Mr. Gomez is..."
I want nothing! Leave me alone.
I want you off my phone. I don't want you to call me again, either.
I... listen, if you don't get off the phone and you call me again, I'm gonna call the police.
[accented] I'm an extravagant man, that's what I am.
[accented] Now, look... do you want wealth?
This is not your phone number... what do you want me to do when people call?
[accented] Travel the world? What do you want?
I don't want anything, I just don't want you to call me. If people call for you, I just say this is the wrong number.
[accented] I've been all around the world, you know.
[accented] I'm a very, very, uh... elegant man.
What do you want from me? What do you want me to do, when people call?
[accented] I would like you to come work for my business.
[scoffs] Yeah, right. I'm... I, I'm hanging up, and I don't wish for you to call back here.
[accented] What do you want, jewels? Gold? What do you want?
